title: mentha canescens subvar. hirsutior
intro: Mentha canescens subvar. hirsutior, also known as Woolly Pennyroyal or Hairy Pennyroyal, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family, characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. The plant is rich in pulegone and menthone, compounds with known medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include aiding digestion, relieving menstrual cramps, and showing potential in reducing the risk of certain neurodegenerative diseases. In herbal preparations, Mentha canescens subvar. hirsutior is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be carefully regulated due to potential toxicity.
